Merchants can of course also manage metafields via a variety of third-party app, achieving the same results via a different interface."}]}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Everybody’s talking about Shopify metafields these days - and with good reason. Use these handy little data sets in the right way and you’re ensuring your ecommerce store has the cleanest and most accurate handling of product data possible."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Merchandising Cloud has been working with metafields for years now, having long ago bet big on their eventual explosion in popularity."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "What’s more, one of our biggest infrastructural developments of 2022, Active Sync, featured a heavy emphasis on metafields."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Essentially a big data ETL pipeline built on top of the Apache Pulsar streaming platform, Active Sync all but revolutionized the speed of ecommerce assortment synchronization for Shopify merchants."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Utilizing Shopify webhooks, all product data is now synchronized, and kept up-to-date, with lightning-fast speeds. This includes changes to prices, products, metafields, and everything in between. Updates take place as soon as the merchant makes a change – with NO requirement for them to manually trigger a sync or wait for the scheduled event. This allows for faster, more efficient synchronization."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Also in 2022, ecommerce platform Shopify released Online Store 2.0. As part of this, Shopify revealed they would also place greater emphasis on metafields - marking that the long-awaited rise of the metafields is imminent "}, {"text": "(if not already upon us)", "italic": true}, {"text": "."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Merchandising Cloud developers welcomed this improvement with open arms, as it meant a closer alignment with the way Merchandising Cloud already operated - our infrastructure was already optimized to sync metafields at lightning-fast speeds."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Using metafields instead of, let’s say, tags, also benefits the merchant - in more ways than one. You now have the potential to far more easily create a rich product data catalog, and the ability to more easily populate specific data for front-end display"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}, {"type": "h3", "children": [{"text": "Ok, but what exactly ARE metafields?"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Metafields are data sets that can be attached to products, variants, customers, collections, and other objects in your online store. They enable you to customize the functionality and appearance of your store by letting you save specialized information that isn't captured within Shopify’s default fields."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "In many ways, from a user perspective, metafields represent an upgrade of what it was like working with tags - with this updated way of working adding increased granularity, a cleaner data structure, and improved performance. In addition, metafields help overcome the limitation of only being able to work on a product level."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "You can use metafields for internal tracking, or to display specialized information on your online store in a variety of ways. You can change the colors of certain CTA buttons, restrict certain products from sale, group and ungroup products in the product grid… and much, much more."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "To get really in-depth, check out the list below. Here you’ll find a number of specific use cases we have identified where metafields are making our merchants’ ecommerce lives that much easier."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Some of these use cases require the merchant to set up, and then populate a metafield "}, {"text": "(or multiple metafields)", "italic": true}, {"text": " and then carry out further actions within the Merchandising Cloud merchant dashboard in order to activate. However other use cases require the merchant to set up and populate metafields, and then contact us via support@maropost .com so that we can activate on your behalf."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Once a metafield has been activated by Merchandising Cloud, the merchant can add the metafields to further products/variants/collections retrospectively, and can also update the data contained in the metafield as needed, without having to contact us a second time."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}, {"type": "h1", "children": [{"text": "PRODUCT CARD PRESENTATION: Custom Sticker Creation"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Adding stickers to product cards is a well-established technique to help shoppers make buying decisions, thereby increasing store conversion."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "This incredibly powerful ecommerce tool "}, {"text": "(which is also sometimes referred to as the displaying of product badges, labels, tags, or markers)", "italic": true}, {"text": " is utilized by all major shopping portals, including Amazon, Walmart, Etsy, and AliExpress to boost sales."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "While there are a number of third-parties offering product sticker services, Merchandising Cloud clients have the additional benefit of sticker creation without the need to download additional apps."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Previously, the creation of customized stickers required the use of some complex tag logic. Now, by using metafields instead of tags, clean and dedicated fields can be used."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "With metafields, merchants can fully define styling in e.g. the text, background, and color of the sticker "}, {"text": "(as well as add any symbol they wish)", "italic": true}, {"text": " for desired products."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}, {"type": "h3", "children": [{"text": "Step by Step: Custom Stickers using metafields"}]}, {"type": "h3", "children": [{"text": "Creating a metafield:"}]}, {"type": "ol", "children": [{"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "Go to your Shopify dashboard."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "In the bottom left corner of the screen, click on ‘Settings’."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "You will now see a list of options along the left hand side of the screen. Click on ‘Metafields’."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "Now select whether you want a product metafield, or a variant metafield. In this case, you would most likely want to choose ‘Product’."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "After you make your selection, you will see a list of all the metafields that have already been created. To create a new one, click ‘Add definition’ "}, {"text": "(which can be found in the top right of the screen)", "italic": true}, {"text": "."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "Fill in the appropriate data such as the name of the metafield, and a description of the metafield. Then select the most appropriate field such as ‘Color’, ‘Single line text’, ‘File’, etc. "}, {"text": "(Note: Merchants must set up individual metafields for each aspect of the sticker that they want to control.
